subject: Sociology
title: Anti-Immigration Movements
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Anti-Immigration Movements.

hook.text: Build the wall. Close the borders. Take our country back. Fear of migration reshapes politics worldwide.

buildup.text: Anti-immigration movements argue that migration threatens national identity, security, jobs, and social cohesion.

discovery.text: Research consistently shows immigrants contribute more in taxes than they consume in services in most countries.

twist.text: Anti-immigration sentiment often rises during economic downturns, when people seek scapegoats for structural problems.

climax.text: Anti-immigration politics reveals how economic anxiety gets redirected into cultural fear and xenophobia.

punchline.text: Blame the newcomer, not the system.

quiz.question: When does anti-immigration sentiment typically rise?
options: During economic downturns when people seek scapegoats
